name: ec2
context: Launch, manage, and terminate EC2 virtual machine instances
info: Amazon EC2 (Elastic Compute Cloud) provides resizable virtual machine instances in the cloud. Choose from a wide range of instance types optimized for compute, memory, storage, or GPU workloads. EC2 instances run Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) — pre-configured OS templates. Security groups act as virtual firewalls controlling inbound/outbound traffic. Key pairs provide SSH access. Elastic IPs provide static public addresses. The AWS SDK v3 `@aws-sdk/client-ec2` package provides RunInstances, DescribeInstances, TerminateInstances, and other commands. Use [[aws/iam]] for instance profiles and [[aws/cloudformation]] for infrastructure-as-code deployments.
triggers: start a virtual machine on aws

sections.title: Launch instances
sections.body: Use `RunInstancesCommand` with an AMI ID, instance type, key pair, and security groups. Tag instances for identification. Use `DescribeInstancesCommand` to check state. Instances launch in `pending` state and transition to `running`. Assign IAM instance profiles via [[aws/iam#roles]] for service access without hardcoded credentials.

sections.title: Instance types
sections.body: General purpose (t3, m6i) for balanced workloads. Compute optimized (c6i, c7g) for CPU-bound tasks. Memory optimized (r6i, r7g) for [[aws/rds]]-like in-memory workloads. Storage optimized (i3, d3) for high I/O. Accelerated (p4d, g5) for ML and GPU. Use `t3.micro` for free tier. Graviton-based (g suffix) instances offer better price-performance.

sections.title: Security groups
sections.body: Security groups are stateful firewalls. Inbound rules control incoming traffic; outbound rules control outgoing (default: allow all outbound). Restrict SSH to known IPs. Open only required ports. Security groups can reference other security groups for internal communication. Define them in [[aws/cloudformation]] for reproducibility.

sections.title: Key pairs and SSH access
sections.body: Create key pairs with `CreateKeyPairCommand`. Save the private key PEM file and set permissions to 400. Specify the key pair name when launching instances. Use `ssh -i key.pem ec2-user@public-ip` to connect. For Windows instances, use the key pair to decrypt the administrator password.

sections.title: Elastic IPs
sections.body: Allocate Elastic IPs with `AllocateAddressCommand`. Associate with an instance using `AssociateAddressCommand`. Elastic IPs are free when associated with a running instance. Unassociated Elastic IPs incur charges. Use Elastic IPs for instances that need a stable public address across restarts.
